upstream_note: v2: full 6x6. Top-level cells are the openai-egress row for v1 compatibility; the full grid is under upstreams.<egress>.cells keyed by ingress dialect.
capability_note: advisory context only (probe-first: every cell is probed regardless): archgw 0.3.22 translates openai-chat ingress natively only to Bedrock Converse; Anthropic egress rides Anthropic's OpenAI-compat surface (id.rs maps (Anthropic, OpenAIChatCompletions) -> OpenAIChatCompletions, never native /v1/messages), Responses is ingress-only, and Gemini/Cohere have no native module; those cells are grey by that capability limit

openai.verdict_note: HTTP 200, passthrough (same dialect, no translation required); round trip to the mock's openai endpoint confirmed

openai-responses.verdict_note: HTTP 200 on POST /v1/responses: bad not JSON

openai.verdict_note: UNTRANSLATED passthrough byte-identical to the mock's canned openai body; the gateway proxied /v1/chat/completions through verbatim instead of translating (HTTP 200)
openai.probe_note: probe failed: HTTP 200; response was the upstream's openai body proxied verbatim, not a translation (passthrough byte-identical to the mock's canned openai body)

anthropic.verdict_note: HTTP 200 with a valid anthropic envelope, but the gateway did not speak the openai-responses dialect to the upstream: the mock received the request on the openai endpoint instead
anthropic.probe_note: probe failed: HTTP 200; upstream request landed on the openai endpoint, not the openai-responses endpoint (this pairing is not wired to a openai-responses upstream)

hardware: AWS m7g.4xlarge (Graviton3, 16 cores / 64 GB). Gateway-under-test pinned to 4 cores (the comparable basis); mock and load generator on 6 cores each so the mock never bottlenecks the streaming sweep. Ubuntu 24.04. One dedicated box per gateway.
